More are expected to join before the finale in November.

items.title: Debunking claims Israeli Jews are 'stealing land' in Morocco
items.description: Internet users have been sharing a video online claiming to ‘prove’ that Israeli Jews are "stealing land" in Morocco, taking back land from before they emigrated. The video shows clashes breaking out in a crowd, though it's difficult to decipher what's taking place or where it is. Our fact-checking unit the FRANCE 24 Observers investigated these claims, and the images are being shared out of context. Vedika Bahl clears up the facts in this episode of Truth or Fake.

items.title: Israel says will reduce French defence imports 'to zero'

According to the French parliament, Paris does not export weapons to Israel, but components to be used in defence systems or to be re-exported to third countries. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.description: New Fundación Proa exhibition, organised with Dia Art Foundation, brings works by a group of key 20th-century contemporary artists to Argentina for the first time. Expect a sensory experience centred on perception, light and space. Leer más

items.title: South Africa deploys 2,200 soldiers as gang violence plagues Cape flats
items.description: South African soldiers moved into gang-ridden Cape Town townships on Wednesday, as two people were killed in fresh violence, nearly 50 days after the president ordered the deployment.

items.title: News24 | Zimbabwe split as public weighs electoral reforms bill
items.description: Zimbabwe has begun nationwide public hearings on a proposed bill that could extend President Mnangagwa’s term and overhaul the electoral system. Supporters call it stabilising, but critics warn it threatens democracy.

items.title: U.K. can lead Strait’s opening, Starmer says, as transatlantic ties fray
items.description: This is not our war and we will not be drawn into the conflict that is not in our national interest, Starmer said, reiterating U.K.’s position of staying out of the U.S.-Israel war on Iran

items.title: Russia wants to legalize cars stolen in EU
items.description: Vehicles stolen in the EU may soon be legally registered in Russia. One German police union fears this could lead to an increase in car thefts in Europe.

items.title: DR Congo fans celebrate historic World Cup qualification after 52 years
items.description: The achievement marks a long-awaited return, with the nation last appearing at the tournament in 1974 under the name Zaire.

items.title: Neo-Nazi figure charged with threats to 'rhetorically rape' MP granted bail
items.description: Neo-Nazi Joel Davis has been released from custody on bail after a Sydney court heard he only gets to shower once every four days while on remand.

items.title: Oil surge elicits tough love from Trump’s Latin American allies
items.description: The crisis in the Strait of Hormuz is rippling beyond Asia. Though Latin America is far from the key oil and gas artery that’s been mostly choked off by Iran, it’s still heavily exposed to oil price volatility. Leer más

items.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
items.description: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

items.title: Artemis II launches historic flyby lunar mission with diverse crew
items.description: Four astronauts – three Americans and one Canadian – blasted off Wednesday on NASA’s Artemis II, humanity’s first lunar flight in over 50 years. The 32-storey rocket soared from Kennedy Space Center, marking the start of a decade-long plan for sustainable Moon exploration and a future landing near the south pole.

items.title: Open wounds

items.description: Israel’s emergency medical service said 14 people, including an 11-year-old girl, were wounded during a missile attack that the military blamed on Iran, and police said caused damage at several sites.

items.title: Céline Dion announces emotional Paris comeback after health battle
items.description: Under the lights of the Eiffel Tower, French-Canadian superstar Céline Dion has shared a powerful comeback announcement. Under the lights of the Eiffel Tower, French-Canadian superstar Céline Dion has shared a powerful comeback announcement.

items.title: Touadera sworn in for third term as Central African Republic president

items.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again
items.description: Having barely budged the previous night, the whale stranded off Germany's Baltic coast for a week finally moved again late Monday. But after showing signs of deterioration, experts aren't sure where it's going next.

items.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

items.title: EU cohesion in the Creuse by numbers
items.description: Renaud Lefort highlights small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) striving to grow with the help of European funds. In rural areas, some projects exist solely because of EU support.

items.title: ‘Sadism is not a political ideology nor a strategy of war, but a moral perversion’
items.description: Populism, on the left and on the right, has wrecked havoc, helping to exacerbate the decline of traditional democratic values. It’s a shame that the defence of human rights has become fodder for culture wars and polarisation. Leer más

items.description: “Captain Ibrahim Traore should be impartially investigated for criminal liability for all abuses by the Burkinabe military and VDPs,” says HRW report

items.title: Oil price steadies for now but cost of petrol soars around the world
items.description: The price of a barrel of oil has steadied with signs the US is open to ending the war against Iran, even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. But at the petrol pump, consumers are still paying the price for the global supply chain disruption.

items.title: News24 | BBC defends action in new sex abuse scandal

items.title: Spanish Police uncover high-tech drug tunnel beneath Ceuta border
items.description: Spanish police have uncovered a highly sophisticated drug-smuggling tunnel in Ceuta, right along the border with Morocco, and it’s unlike anything you’d expect from a typical trafficking route.

items.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Monday voiced hope for working with elements within Iran’s government, saying the United States privately had received positive messages.

items.title: With or without Milei
items.description: Milei has given Vaca Muerta a push, but growth there precedes and will outlive his Presidency. It is arguably the only consensus policy the country has had for the past decade and a half. Its development momentum will not stop anytime soon. Leer más

items.title: Premature babies evacuated from Gaza strip return to their parents
items.description: A group of toddlers evacuated as premature infants just a month into the Israel-Hamas war returned Monday to families overjoyed yet worried about their futures in a transformed Gaza.

items.title: South Africans load up on fuel as Iran war leads to major price hike
items.description: South African motorists were on Tuesday headed to gas stations in large numbers in anticipation of record fuel hikes that will come into effect at midnight as the world continues to feel impacts of the Iran war that has affected global oil and fuel prices.

items.title: News24 | Trump threatens to bomb Iran ‘back to the Stone Ages where they belong’
items.description: The United States will carry out aggressive strikes on Iran over the next two to three weeks and is nearing completion of its main strategic objectives in the war, US President Donald Trump said in a prime-time address to the nation.

items.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
items.description: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

items.description: President Daniel Noboa has led a hardline effort against local criminal networks, including military operations with US.

items.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
items.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

items.title: 'Like a camping trip, but everything needs to be attached'
items.description: NASA is set to launch four astronauts as soon as Wednesday evening on a 10-day flight around the moon, marking the most ambitious U.S. space mission in decades. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with astronaut Jean-François Clervoy, Founder of Air Zero G, about what life is going to be like for astronauts on board the Artemis II. He says the mission capsule is a very confined space and the four astronauts "better get along with each other".

items.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
items.description: Lunar discoveries and a space race with China is seeing the US invest time and money to get to the Moon - and beyond.

items.title: Live: Collingwood looking to exact revenge on Brisbane in blockbuster clash
items.description: After being eliminated by Brisbane in last year's preliminary final, Collingwood is looking to inflict some pain on the reigning AFL premiers. Follow live.

items.title: Haiti: At least 30 people dead after gang attacks town
items.description: Gran Grif attacked Petite-Rivière de l’Artibonite early Sunday, burning homes and leaving bodies strewn on the streets. The gang attacked again on Monday, said Bertide Horace, spokesperson for the Commission for Dialogue, Reconciliation and Awareness to Save the Artibonite, an activist group

items.title: Sam Altman's sister amends lawsuit accusing OpenAI CEO of sexual abuse
items.description: Sam Altman’s defamation claim stemmed from social media posts by his sister that referred to alleged abuse

items.title: Trump claims Iran wants a ceasefire, Iran says remarks are ‘false and baseless’
items.description: Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has asked the United States for a ceasefire -- adding that Washington would only consider it when the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Tehran, though, has denied this -- the Foreign Ministry calling Trump's remarks "baseless." It comes hours ahead of the president's primetime address to the nation. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

items.title: South Africans react as fuel prices surge despite tax relief
items.description: Authorities announced a reduction of the general fuel levy by three rand per litre, a measure aimed at cushioning the impact of soaring global oil prices linked to the Iran war. However, the relief has done little to offset one of the steepest pump price increases on record.

items.title: US Supreme Court debates citizenship, with rare Trump visit
items.description: Donald Trump is the first president to appear at a Supreme Court oral argument, as he pushes to end birthright citizenship in the US.
